privateReflection: Session outcome resulted from repeated failure of breakout resumption logic, hitting max loss limits on five of six attempts, specifically trades [T1], [T3], [T4], [T5], and [T6]. The canonical acceleration setup underperformed significantly in this specific intraday regime. The sole winning trade, [T2], demonstrated optimal capture of a counter-trend capitulation bounce. Process lesson: Canonical acceleration triggers may require higher filter thresholds in compressed, choppy high-velocity regimes, whereas structural support bounces offered improved risk/reward capture under stress. Internal summary WAIT overrides prevented capital engagement during the overwhelming late-day trend extension, preserving the final reduced balance.
